description: Benchmark tests for arch-common skill - Shared configuration for architecture Skills defining L0/L1/L2 layer system and naming conventions

baseline_comparison.description: Teams independently deciding on architecture doc naming and placement without shared standards

evals.name: layer-hierarchy-definition
evals.prompt: Explain the L0/L1/L2 layer system defined in arch-common. What does each layer represent and what kind of documentation belongs at each level?

assertions.name: defines_l0_as_project
assertions.description: L0 should be defined as project-level documentation

assertions.name: defines_l1_as_module
assertions.description: L1 should be defined as module-level documentation

assertions.name: defines_l2_as_component
assertions.description: L2 should be defined as component-level documentation

assertions.name: explains_hierarchy_relationship
assertions.description: Should explain the parent-child relationship between layers

assertions.name: provides_concrete_examples
assertions.description: Should provide concrete file path examples for each layer

evals.name: naming-convention
evals.prompt: What are the architecture document naming conventions defined by arch-common? How should I name a project-level doc vs a module-level doc vs a component-level doc?

setup.context: user wants to create new architecture docs

assertions.name: specifies_architecture_md_for_l0
assertions.description: L0 project-level doc should be named ARCHITECTURE.md

assertions.name: specifies_arch_md_extension_for_l1_l2
assertions.description: L1/L2 docs should use *.arch.md naming pattern

assertions.name: gives_naming_examples
assertions.description: Should give concrete naming examples (e.g. auth.arch.md, payment.arch.md)

assertions.name: explains_case_convention
assertions.description: Should clarify case convention (uppercase ARCHITECTURE.md vs lowercase *.arch.md)

evals.name: doc-location-rules
evals.prompt: Where should architecture documentation be placed for a project with modules at src/auth/, src/payment/, and src/notifications/? Apply arch-common placement rules.

assertions.name: places_l0_at_root
assertions.description: L0 ARCHITECTURE.md should be placed at the project root

assertions.name: places_l1_inside_module
assertions.description: L1 arch docs should be placed inside their respective module directories

assertions.name: covers_all_modules
assertions.description: Should specify doc location for all three modules

assertions.name: explains_colocation_principle
assertions.description: Should explain that docs live alongside the code they describe

evals.name: cross-skill-compatibility
evals.prompt: How does arch-common integrate with arch-search and arch-update skills? Explain the shared configuration points.

assertions.name: mentions_arch_search_integration
assertions.description: Should explain how arch-common config is used by arch-search

assertions.name: mentions_arch_update_integration
assertions.description: Should explain how arch-common config is used by arch-update

assertions.name: identifies_shared_config
assertions.description: Should identify the shared configuration elements (layer definitions, naming rules)

assertions.name: explains_consistency_benefit
assertions.description: Should explain why shared config ensures consistent behavior across skills
